board.title: Classical Natural Language Processing
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Natural language processing exists at the intersection of linguistics and computer science. The early foundations took shape in the 1940s and accelerated in the 1990s as research shifted from rule-based systems toward statistical language modeling.

clues.question: Natural language processing subfield focused on interpreting meaning in language beyond surface-level syntax
Smallest unit of sound that distinguishes meaning between spoken words in a language; foundational to speech recognition
Formalized language as a structured rule-based system in his 1957 landmark paper "Syntactic Structures"; often called the father of modern linguistics
Tree representation showing the syntactic structure of a string according to a context-free grammar
Early natural language processing program developed at MIT in 1964; simulated conversation by reflecting users' statements back as questions
Process of reducing inflected words to their base dictionary form, or lemma; e.g. "studies" → "study"
Published "Computing Machinery and Intelligence" in 1950, introducing a test for whether computers could convincingly imitate human language
Established the idea that language could be modeled statistically in his 1948 paper "A Mathematical Theory of Communication"; 1972 Turing Award winner
Process of splitting raw text into smaller units called tokens before language analysis
Process of reducing words to their root form by removing prefixes and suffixes; e.g. "studies" → "studi"
Collection of authentic text or audio used to analyze patterns in language
Probabilistic model where future states depend on the current state; foundational to statistical language modeling
Contiguous sequence of N words or characters used in statistical language modeling and text prediction
